metadata.description: Comprehensive Pharmacy Intelligence Dataset for ML-Powered Recommendation System

dosage_strengths.note: Dosage strengths are grouped by medicine type. Select based on medicine type dispensed.

usage_guidelines.note: Usage guidelines are grouped by medicine type. Apply the relevant set when dispensing.
Tablet_Capsule: Take with a full glass of water
Take after meals to reduce GI irritation (NSAIDs, antibiotics, iron)
Take on an empty stomach 30-60 min before meals (PPIs, Levothyroxine)
Do not crush or chew enteric-coated or SR tablets
Take at the same time daily for chronic medications
Complete the full antibiotic course even if symptoms improve
Do not double-dose if a dose is missed - skip and continue schedule
Swallow capsules whole with water - do not open unless instructed
Store at room temperature away from sunlight and moisture
Space Calcium + Thyroid medication doses by at least 4 hours
Do not stop blood pressure, thyroid or diabetes medicines without doctor advice
Syrup: Use the measuring spoon or cup provided for accurate dosing
Do not dilute unless specifically instructed
Refrigerate after opening if label advises
Give children only paediatric-appropriate doses per weight or age
Do not share the measuring spoon between patients
Inhaler: Shake MDI canister well before each use
Exhale fully before placing mouthpiece in mouth
Press canister down firmly as you begin to inhale slowly and deeply
Hold breath for 10 seconds after inhalation then exhale slowly
Rinse mouth with water after using corticosteroid inhaler to prevent oral thrush
Clean mouthpiece weekly with a dry cloth
Use a spacer device for children and elderly patients
Keep rescue inhaler accessible at all times
Monitor dose counter and replace inhaler before reaching zero
Wait 1 minute between puffs if more than one dose is prescribed
Cream_Gel_Ointment: Apply a thin layer to the affected area only
Wash hands thoroughly before and after application
Do not apply to broken or infected skin unless it is a wound-specific product
Avoid contact with eyes, nostrils, and mouth
Do not cover with airtight dressing unless specifically advised
Apply 2-3 times daily or as directed on the label
Do not use potent corticosteroid cream on face, groin, or underarms long-term
For antifungal creams, continue for 7 days after symptoms resolve to prevent relapse
Do not stop steroid cream abruptly after prolonged use - taper as advised
Eye_Drops: Wash hands thoroughly before instilling eye drops
Tilt head back and gently pull lower eyelid down to create a pocket
Do not let dropper tip touch the eye or any surface to prevent contamination
Wait at least 5 minutes between instillation of different eye drops
Press gently on the inner corner of the eye for 1 minute after instillation
Discard opened bottle after 28 days even if solution remains
Remove contact lenses before use and reinsert after 15 minutes
Store in a cool dry place - some formulations require refrigeration
Ear_Drops: Warm the bottle gently in your hands for 1-2 minutes before use
Lie down with the affected ear facing upward
For adults pull earlobe gently back and upward to straighten the canal
For children pull earlobe gently back and downward
Instill the prescribed number of drops and remain in position for 5-10 minutes
Plug ear lightly with a small cotton ball if instructed
Do not use ear drops if eardrum perforation is suspected unless specifically prescribed
Complete the full prescribed course even if symptoms resolve early
Nasal_Spray_Drops: Blow nose gently before use to clear nasal passages
Shake nasal spray before each use
Prime the spray pump before first use by pressing several times
Tilt head slightly forward during instillation for nasal drops
Breathe in gently through the nose while pressing the spray
Do not tilt head back after spraying to avoid swallowing
Do not use decongestant nasal spray for more than 3-5 days - risk of rebound congestion
Clean the nasal spray tip with a clean tissue after each use
Do not share nasal sprays between patients
Injection: Administer only by a trained healthcare professional
Verify dosage strength and check patient allergy history before administration
Use strict aseptic technique at the injection site
Rotate injection sites for insulin - use abdomen, thigh or upper arm
Do not share needles or syringes under any circumstances
Discard used needles immediately in a sharps disposal container
Observe patient for at least 30 minutes after injection for allergic reactions
Check insulin vial for clarity - do not use if cloudy or discoloured unless expected
Powder_Sachet_Oral_Liquid: Dissolve the sachet in the prescribed volume of clean drinking water only
Do not use fruit juice, milk, or carbonated beverages for dissolving ORS
Stir thoroughly until the powder is fully dissolved before consuming
Consume the prepared solution within 1 hour of preparation
Store unmixed sachets at room temperature in a dry place
For ORS in diarrhoea give small sips frequently rather than large amounts at once
Offer children ORS solution using a spoon or cup not a bottle
Lotion_Shampoo: For shampoo wet hair thoroughly then apply and massage into the scalp
Leave medicated shampoo on for 3-5 minutes before rinsing thoroughly
For body lotion or scabicide apply to the entire body from neck downward on clean dry skin
Leave scabicide lotion on for 8-12 hours typically overnight then wash off
Do not apply to the face or mucous membranes unless specifically instructed
Wash all clothing bedding and towels in hot water after scabicide treatment
Repeat scabicide application after 7 days if advised
Mouthwash: Do not eat or drink for at least 30 minutes before and after using mouthwash
Measure the correct volume using the cap provided
Swish vigorously in the mouth for 30 seconds then spit out - do not swallow
Dilute with equal amount of water if mouth is sensitive
Do not use chlorhexidine mouthwash for more than 2 consecutive weeks
Avoid using immediately after brushing teeth to preserve effectiveness

side_effects.effect: Warm or Numb Injection Site Feeling
Bad Taste in Mouth Post Inhaler
Chemical Burn from Improper Antiseptic Use

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Medicine ineffective after 3 days of expected response
suspicious_health_issues.action: Review diagnosis; consider alternative medicine or referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Same medicine batch causing adverse reactions in multiple patients
suspicious_health_issues.action: Flag batch for investigation; notify drug authority

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Symptom worsening despite correct treatment
suspicious_health_issues.action: Rule out drug resistance or wrong diagnosis; refer to physician

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusual side effect not listed in standard drug profile
suspicious_health_issues.action: Document and report to ADR monitoring cell

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient allergic reaction to first dose
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop medicine; document allergy in record; provide safe alternative

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Severe GI bleeding after NSAID use
suspicious_health_issues.action: Immediate hospital referral; stop NSAID permanently

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Recurrent UTI despite completing antibiotic course
suspicious_health_issues.action: Urine culture and sensitivity; change antibiotic class

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Blood sugar uncontrolled on maximum oral dose
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refer to physician for insulin initiation review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient returned with jaundice after antibiotic use
suspicious_health_issues.action: LFT testing; discontinue suspect drug; hepatologist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Multiple patients reporting rash from same medicine lot number
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine batch; report to manufacturer and drug regulator

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient purchasing same medicine from multiple pharmacy branches
suspicious_health_issues.action: Patient counselling; inform treating doctor

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Antibiotic dispensed without prescription returning with worsening infection
suspicious_health_issues.action: Enforce Rx policy strictly; refer patient to doctor

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Paediatric patient given adult dose (pharmacist error)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Immediate dose review and correction; internal error report

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Expired medicine found in dispensed pack reported by patient
suspicious_health_issues.action: Recall entire lot from shelf; reimburse patient; audit stock

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient reports counterfeit packaging (different colour or texture)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine; report to drug controller; send sample for testing

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unexpected neurological symptom matching medication side effect profile
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop medicine; neurology referral; document ADR

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Anaphylactic shock post-injection at pharmacy
suspicious_health_issues.action: Administer epinephrine immediately; call emergency services; document

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Corticosteroid cream causing facial skin thinning from long-term use
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop steroid cream on face; taper; dermatologist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient showing signs of benzodiazepine dependence with monthly repeat requests
suspicious_health_issues.action: Flag in system; psychiatry referral; taper plan required

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Hypoglycaemia episode after sulfonylurea in elderly patient
suspicious_health_issues.action: Administer glucose; review and reduce dose; physician review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Medicine disintegrating or showing unusual odour in pack
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense; quarantine; report to supplier immediately

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Pregnant patient given Category D or X drug unknowingly
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop immediately; obstetric referral; document incident

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Renal patient given nephrotoxic NSAID without renal check
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop NSAID; substitute paracetamol; nephrology review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Cluster of fever cases unresponsive to standard antipyretics in same locality
suspicious_health_issues.action: Notify public health authority; report cluster data

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Child given tetracycline by parent (contraindicated under 8 years)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop drug immediately; monitor teeth; dentist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: High frequency of same disease diagnosis from single zone within short period
suspicious_health_issues.action: Report to local health authority for epidemiological investigation

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusual or non-standard lot number format on medicine packaging
suspicious_health_issues.action: Verify with manufacturer; quarantine stock pending investigation

suspicious_health_issues.issue: QR code on pack scanning to incorrect or unknown manufacturer
suspicious_health_issues.action: Remove from shelf; verify entire stock; report to drug regulator

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Drug interaction detected by system during dispensing review
suspicious_health_issues.action: Alert pharmacist; consult physician before proceeding with dispensing

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient on warfarin requesting ibuprofen or NSAID OTC
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense NSAID; recommend paracetamol; contact prescriber

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Insulin vial appears cloudy when it should be clear (degradation suspected)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense; quarantine vial; replace from verified new stock

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Children syrup colour or smell differs noticeably from previous batch
suspicious_health_issues.action: Withhold batch; send for testing; report to QC team

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Persistent dry cough lasting over 8 weeks in patient on ACE inhibitor
suspicious_health_issues.action: Switch to ARB class; document outcome and notify prescriber

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient vomiting within 15 minutes of oral dose (absorption failure)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Assess for repeat dose; consider alternative route; physician input

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Repeated prescription for same antibiotic without culture review
suspicious_health_issues.action: Recommend culture sensitivity test; alert prescribing physician

suspicious_health_issues.issue: OTC antifungal repeat purchase every week suggesting systemic cause
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refer to physician; rule out diabetes or immunodeficiency

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Skin whitening steroid cream sold OTC causing facial complications
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel patient; stop product; dermatologist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient presenting with overdose symptoms after self-medication
suspicious_health_issues.action: Emergency referral; do not dispense further; document incident

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Temperature-sensitive medicine stored improperly (cold chain break)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense; check temperature log; discard if breached

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Multiple patients from same household with identical rare adverse effect
suspicious_health_issues.action: Investigate shared medicine batch; report to pharmacovigilance

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient age under 12 requesting prescription-only medicine OTC
suspicious_health_issues.action: Decline dispensing; counsel parent; refer to paediatrician

suspicious_health_issues.issue: High-dose corticosteroid use over 4 weeks without physician oversight
suspicious_health_issues.action: Escalate to physician; implement taper protocol

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Pattern of high sedative or anxiolytic requests from specific age group
suspicious_health_issues.action: Flag in system; pharmacy management review; may require reporting

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Inhaler technique error causing inadequate asthma control
suspicious_health_issues.action: Re-demonstrate technique; consider spacer; reassess control

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Same patient purchasing narcotics from multiple pharmacy branches
suspicious_health_issues.action: Cross-pharmacy flag; refuse dispensing; report to drug controller

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Batch recall received from manufacturer while stock still on pharmacy shelf
suspicious_health_issues.action: Immediately quarantine and return recalled batch; notify patients

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusual spike in antidiarrheal sales in one zone suggesting local outbreak
suspicious_health_issues.action: Report to local health authority for epidemiological assessment

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Medicine sold as generic equivalent but with different composition
suspicious_health_issues.action: Remove from dispensing; pharmacist retraining; update database

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient with active hepatitis on hepatotoxic drug combination
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop hepatotoxic drug; hepatologist referral; monitor LFT

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Elderly patient on multiple CNS depressants from different prescribers
suspicious_health_issues.action: Comprehensive medication review; alert all prescribing physicians

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Drug temperature exceeded 30 degrees C during transport (heat damage)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine stock; verify manufacturer stability data before use

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Batch showing unusual opacity in liquid formulation
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense; separate batch; report to QC team

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Prescription appears illegible or suspected to be forged
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refuse dispensing; verify with prescribing doctor; report to authority

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Child given ORS incorrectly diluted with too little water (hypernatremia risk)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Correct immediately; monitor child; educate parent on preparation

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient developed tendon pain after fluoroquinolone course (tendinopathy)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop fluoroquinolone; orthopedic review; document ADR report

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Antiviral medication given after Day 5 of symptom onset (too late)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel patient on timing; audit prescribing practice

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Multiple anaphylaxis events tied to same vaccine or injection batch
suspicious_health_issues.action: Report to health ministry; quarantine batch; activate pharmacovigilance

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient develops Stevens-Johnson syndrome post sulfonamide use
suspicious_health_issues.action: Immediate hospitalisation; stop drug; allergy documented permanently

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Prescription drug sold without Rx check caught in system audit
suspicious_health_issues.action: Staff retraining; enforce mandatory Rx check; system audit review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Pain reliever ineffective across 3 consecutive visits suggesting systemic cause
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refer to physician urgently; cancer or systemic screening protocol

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient on thyroid medication requesting stimulants or weight loss pills
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel patient; pharmacist review; notify physician

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unexplained tachycardia in multiple patients on same bronchodilator batch
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine batch; pharmacovigilance alert; arrange batch testing

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patients returning with skin darkening after topical hydroquinone or steroid combo
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop product; dermatologist referral; review product safety

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusually high product returns for a specific medicine lot
suspicious_health_issues.action: Analyse return data; contact manufacturer QC department

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient on blood thinner requesting ibuprofen OTC for pain relief
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense ibuprofen; recommend paracetamol; advise prescriber

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Chronic antacid user presenting with vitamin B12 deficiency symptoms
suspicious_health_issues.action: Supplement B12; review PPI necessity; gastroenterologist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient develops liver failure after herbal supplement combined with prescription drug
suspicious_health_issues.action: Emergency referral; document herbal supplement; update patient record

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Insulin pen device malfunction leading to incorrect dosing
suspicious_health_issues.action: Replace device; check dosing history; report to manufacturer

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Antimalarial stock shortage causing treatment delay in high-risk zone
suspicious_health_issues.action: Alert procurement; contact alternative supplier; escalate to health authority

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient taking same medicine in two brand names unknowingly (duplicate therapy)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel patient; reconcile all medicines; alert prescriber

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Post-operative patient requesting prescription painkillers without documentation
suspicious_health_issues.action: Verify with surgical team; do not dispense without valid Rx

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Cream or gel showing phase separation or unusual texture change in batch
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine; do not dispense; contact QA department

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Minor (under 18) requesting multiple purchases of sedatives
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refuse dispensing; escalate to pharmacist-in-charge; safeguarding protocol

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient requests emergency contraceptive for third time in one month
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel on regular contraception options; GP referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Inhaler dose counter shows zero but patient says medicine still feels active
suspicious_health_issues.action: Educate on dose counter importance; replace inhaler; re-check technique

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient with known kidney disease requesting NSAID (ibuprofen or diclofenac)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Decline NSAID; recommend paracetamol; notify nephrologist

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Multiple pharmacy branches flagging complaints about same drug manufacturer
suspicious_health_issues.action: Escalate to drug controller; suspend manufacturer lot across all branches

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Inconsistent tablet size within same blister pack reported by patient
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine; report to manufacturer; pharmacy manager review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Oral antibiotic prescribed for clearly viral illness (AMR concern)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Alert prescribing physician; educate on antibiotic stewardship guidelines

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Gel or cream pack seal found broken at point of purchase
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not sell; return to supplier; inspect entire lot

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusual spike in cough syrup sales in a zone more than 3x normal volume
suspicious_health_issues.action: Restrict OTC sales; notify zonal health officer; investigate cause

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient presents hypertensive emergency while currently on antihypertensives
suspicious_health_issues.action: Emergency referral; review medication compliance and drug efficacy

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Topical antibiotic used over 2 weeks causing resistant skin infection
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop topical antibiotic; take swab for culture; dermatologist referral

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Eye drops dispensed for ear use or ear drops dispensed for eye use
suspicious_health_issues.action: Correct immediately; clarify with patient; internal dispensing audit

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient with G6PD deficiency given nitrofurantoin or primaquine
suspicious_health_issues.action: Withdraw drug; G6PD-safe alternative; haematology review

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Adverse event rate at specific pharmacy unusually high in internal audit
suspicious_health_issues.action: Internal audit; staff training; inspection by QA team

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Medicine packaging lacks required regional language labelling as per regulations
suspicious_health_issues.action: Flag to compliance officer; do not dispense; return to supplier

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Customer confused between two similar-looking drug names (LASA error)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Update shelf labelling; add TALL MAN letters; staff education

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Diabetic patient on metformin with lactic acidosis symptoms (confusion and breathing difficulty)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Stop metformin; emergency hospital referral; monitor renal function urgently

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Nasal decongestant requested for daily use beyond 5 days
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel on rhinitis medicamentosa; switch to saline spray; ENT referral if needed

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Skin infection spreading despite 5 days of topical treatment (systemic infection suspected)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Refer to physician for oral or IV antibiotics; wound culture

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unexpected interaction between self-purchased OTC drug and prescribed medication
suspicious_health_issues.action: Medication reconciliation; counsel patient; notify prescriber immediately

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Pharmacist repeatedly dispensing without checking allergy history (system audit flag)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Retrain pharmacist; enforce mandatory allergy check at every dispensing

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Calcium supplement and thyroid medicine dispensed together without spacing warning
suspicious_health_issues.action: Counsel patient to space doses by 4 hours; document interaction in record

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Unusual metallic taste in medicine reported by 3 or more patients from same batch
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine batch; arrange chemical analysis; report to QC

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient using antidepressant plus tramadol simultaneously (serotonin syndrome risk)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Do not dispense combination; contact prescriber; monitor patient closely

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Child accidentally ingested adult blood pressure medicine from home (poisoning)
suspicious_health_issues.action: Poison control referral; emergency care; counsel family on safe storage

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Patient requests medicine associated with national shortage
suspicious_health_issues.action: Notify patient; check approved substitutes; escalate to procurement

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Student requesting cognitive enhancers without valid prescription
suspicious_health_issues.action: Decline dispensing; educate on risks; no prescription means no dispensing

suspicious_health_issues.issue: Batch with smeared print or unreadable manufacturing and expiry date
suspicious_health_issues.action: Quarantine; do not dispense; report defect to manufacturer and drug regulator

sample_transaction_records.usage_instruction: Take after meals; 1 tablet every 4-6 hours; max 4 tablets per day
Dissolve in 200ml clean water; give small sips every few minutes
Take once daily at same time; do not stop without doctor advice
Take once daily; avoid alcohol; may cause mild drowsiness
Take 30 minutes before breakfast; do not crush capsule
